response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior developer, but the profile fundamentally misses this role's hard requirements. There is no indication of Japanese language ability (a native-level requirement), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and no background in network security technologies such as MACsec, 802.1X, or switch-level networking. His C experience is limited to a brief stint over a decade ago; his recent work is web/full-stack TypeScript and DLang. The mismatch is too large for an embedded security firmware position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience, well above the 3-year minimum
Some systems-level and low-latency experience (C/C++ at National Instruments, DLang async I/O at Sociomantic)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and open-source contributions
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — the role requires native-level Japanese
No demonstrated Linux kernel, embedded networking, or security technology experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching)
C for UNIX proficiency is dated and thin; recent career is web/full-stack, not embedded firmware

key_factors.factor: No embedded security/networking or Linux kernel experience
Long, broad software engineering track record
